bullets: Drag your printer tiles into any order again — turn off 'Auto-arrange by status' in the menu, tap the drag-drop button (bottom-left), then drag the tiles around the dashboard (both full and compact tiles) and tap the green check to finish. The tidy masonry layout returns as soon as you're done
Restoring a backup now rebuilds your dashboard to match it exactly — the same printer order and each tile's layout (including which webcams are hidden), not just adding any missing printers

If the phone already has printers that aren't in the backup, restore asks before removing them (and lists them first), so you can't lose a printer you paired after making the backup. On a fresh phone there's nothing to remove, so it just restores
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Show or hide each printer's webcam — the menu's Webcams entry opens a list of your printers, each with a switch. Turn one off and its dashboard tile shrinks to a compact card (name, status and temperatures, no camera), and the dashboard packs the full and compact tiles together so the printers you're not watching take up far less room
A hidden webcam also stops that tile fetching its camera, so it doubles as a per-printer data saver — this replaces the old all-or-nothing 'Show webcams' switch with per-printer control
See-through tiles — on the Custom theme you can now set a tile opacity (0-100) so your dashboard background shows through the tiles, while the camera feed stays solid
More of the app is translated — the Advanced Power Switch and the new tile-opacity controls are now covered in all 8 languages
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Clear a finished print right from the tile — when a job ends, its tile shows a Done (or Cancelled) badge; tap the small × on it to send the printer back to Idle. One tap, no pop-up, and your print notification updates to Idle too

Give the dashboard your own background — switch to the Custom theme, open it from the menu, then pick any image from your phone to sit behind the tiles. It's centred and scaled to fit over your theme colour, so a wallpaper fills the screen and a logo PNG sits neatly in the middle with the theme colour around it
Refreshed icon guide — the in-app guide (menu → About) now covers all the newer tile buttons: Print a file, Macros, Power, Lighting, Camera and Camera setup, plus the new clear-a-print ×

Tidier appearance and menu — the cold-start loading screen now matches the dark theme (dark, with the red Moongate brand mark, instead of flashing white on a light-mode phone), the 'System default' theme option has been removed (themes are now Dark, Light and Custom — default Dark), and Settings + Language scroll with the rest of the menu (only the version stays pinned at the bottom)

Advanced Power Switch (in the menu, beside Lighting) — for printers whose power is a Klipper macro instead of a Moonraker power device: set an On + Off pair or a single toggle macro, and the tile's power button drives the printer through it. With on/off macros it asks 'Turn on' or 'Turn off' rather than assuming the state; the light bulb does the same when it has on/off macros but no toggle or status pin
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Power your printer on or off right from its dashboard tile — if a printer has a Moonraker power device (a [power ...] section, any type: GPIO, Shelly, TP-Link, etc.), a power button appears in the bottom-left corner of its camera. Tap it and confirm to switch the printer on or off

It works even when the printer is off, since Moonraker keeps running — so you can wake a powered-down printer straight from its tile. Powering off is blocked while a print is running, for devices Moonraker locks
Tidier idle tiles — a printer that's offline or sitting idle (Klipper not running) now shows just its name, instead of a meaningless 0°/0° temperature row

New 'Show webcams' switch in the menu (under Camera feed) turns all dashboard camera feeds off or on at once — a quick data saver that also lets on-demand cameras idle. The full-screen camera view still works when you need it
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Your print notification now shows when the print will finish — the projected clock time (e.g. 1:20 PM) sits alongside the time remaining, the same ETA Klipper and Mainsail show. It follows your app language for 12- or 24-hour time

Make the notification your own — a new Notification content screen (under Print notifications in the menu) lets you choose exactly which details show: progress, time remaining, finish time, hotend temperature and bed temperature

Drag them into any order you like and switch off the ones you don't care about — want only the finish time and not the running time? Turn it off. A live preview updates as you tweak, and your choices are saved and travel in your backups
Tidier menu — the Lighting option now sits right under Font size for quicker reach, instead of down in the About section
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Re-opening a printer is now instant — the full Mainsail/Fluidd page stays loaded in the background when you go back to the dashboard, so opening a printer again no longer replays Mainsail's "Initializing…" every time. The difference is biggest over the tunnel, away from home
The first time you open each printer still loads it fresh; after that it's kept warm. If your phone runs low on memory, the least-recently-used pages are released automatically
Control your printer lights from the dashboard — set up which printers have a light under the new Lighting menu, then tap the bulb in the corner of a tile's camera to switch it on or off

Use a single toggle macro, or separate on/off macros — whichever your setup has. Point Moongate at the light's Klipper object (like output_pin caselight) and the bulb shows the real on/off state, even if you switch the light elsewhere
No Pi update needed — just update the app
Squarer camera feeds on the dashboard — each printer tile's live view is now a clean 1:1 square instead of an odd wide, short crop, with the printer's name and temperatures tucked neatly underneath
Offline printers sit flush — an unreachable tile now fills with its Mainsail/Fluidd logo and shows just the name, instead of leaving an empty gap below the text
A tidier view while printing — the macro button hides itself while a print is running, so the progress bar and percentage have room and stay easy to read. It comes back when the printer is idle
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Run your printer's macros from the dashboard — a new macro button (next to the folder) on any online printer tile opens the list of Klipper macros defined on that machine. Tap one to run it, with a quick confirm since some macros heat or move the printer
Star your favourites to pin them to the top — handy when a machine has dozens of macros but you only use a handful. Your stars are saved per printer and included in backups
Helper macros (names starting with _) and Moongate's own pairing macros are hidden, to keep the list to the ones you'd actually run
Tidier tile controls — the firmware-restart button now appears only when a printer has errored (where a restart is the fix), instead of sitting on every idle tile
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Tap to watch a camera full-screen — printer tiles showing a camera now have a small eye button in the corner. Tap it for a centred, full-screen live view you can pinch to zoom (the same view also opens from the camera icon on the printer page)
Turn your phone sideways for a widescreen view — the camera rotates to landscape even if the rest of the app stays in portrait, and goes back when you close it
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Arrange your printers your way — turn off "Auto-arrange by status" in the menu and drag the dashboard tiles into any order you like. Tap the drag-drop button (bottom-left) to start, rearrange the tiles, then tap the green check to lock them in
Your custom order and the auto-arrange choice are saved, and included in backups
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Clearer notification settings — the two notification categories were named backwards. The always-on list of your printers is now "Print status", and the start / finish / cancel pop-ups are now "Print alerts" (they were "Print progress" and "Print status"). Your on/off choices carry over
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Webcam on the printer page now works remotely — open a printer and tap the new camera icon in the top bar for a full-screen live view. An external camera (like a phone used as a webcam) that only loaded at home in the embedded Mainsail page now streams over the tunnel too, the same way the dashboard preview does
A one-time hint points you to it when a printer's camera can't load remotely in the page
No Pi update needed — just update the app
See what's new before you update — the update banner now has a "What's new" button that shows every change from your installed version up to the latest, right in the app
No Pi update needed — just update the app

External network cameras — point a tile at a camera that isn't connected to Klipper, like an old phone used as a webcam. Tap the gear on a tile's camera and enter its address (the same URL you use in Mainsail, e.g. http://192.168.0.107:8080/video)
Cameras already set up in Mainsail are detected automatically — they just appear, and the gear lets you override them
Works on Wi-Fi and remotely through your printer (for cameras on your home network). A "Camera config icons" toggle in the menu hides the gears if you don't use them
Re-run the Pi installer (or update via Mainsail → Software Updates) for auto-detect and remote cameras; a hand-set camera on Wi-Fi works without a Pi update

Clearer reconnect — if the app loses its cloud sign-in (usually rate-limited after several quick reinstalls), the dashboard now shows a "reconnecting, retrying" banner and keeps trying in the background, instead of silently showing every printer offline. Your printers come back on their own
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Re-pairing a printer now reconnects in seconds instead of minutes — the Pi reports its connection to the cloud straight away instead of waiting for its next 5-minute check-in (re-run the Pi installer, or update via Mainsail → Software Updates, to get it)

Clearer pairing-time hint — the GATE-code notice now says the printer can take up to about a minute (it's waiting for the secure tunnel), instead of the old "up to ~10 minutes". Scanning the QR is still an instant local connection
The menu and the Add Printer screen now show a scrollbar, so it's obvious there's more to scroll on smaller screens

Add Printer and rename: tapping a field now reliably re-opens the keyboard after you drop it, and the name and address fields gain a small keyboard button to bring it back — so you can always fix a typo
The moon-gate logo in the dashboard header now sits on a transparent background, so it looks clean against any theme
No Pi update needed — just update the app
Print a stored file from the dashboard — tap the new folder button on a ready printer to browse the G-code files already on it (with slicer thumbnails), pick one and start it
A proper Moongate app icon at last — a red moon-gate on black — across the launcher, the dashboard bar and notifications
Notification fixes — a powered-off printer now shows Offline instead of Idle, and switching between Wi-Fi and cellular no longer re-fires a spurious "print cancelled" alert
Print notifications now refresh the moment you add, remove or restore a printer — no more "No printers" stuck in the notification after a restore
New "Update frequency" setting — choose how often notifications check your printers: 5s, 10s, 15s, 30s or 1 minute (Menu, under Print notifications)
Status now updates promptly when a printer errors or finishes, instead of lagging behind
New alert when a printer recovers and is ready again after an error (e.g. a firmware restart)
No Pi update needed — just update the app

New optional print notifications — turn them on (Menu → Print notifications) for a live status of every printer in your notification shade: per-printer progress, ETA, temperatures and heat-up, plus alerts when a print starts, finishes, pauses or errors. Works with the app in the background; off by default

Backup now keeps your settings too — theme and custom colours, dashboard columns, language and camera refresh ride along with your backup, not just the printer list (your app-lock PIN stays on the device for security)
Printers now sort by activity — whatever's printing floats to the top, then Ready, Idle and Offline, on both the dashboard and the notification
No Pi update needed — just update the app

Moongate now speaks your language — translations for German, French, Spanish, Italian, Simplified Chinese, Russian and Polish, alongside English. Pick your language on first launch, or change it anytime from Language at the bottom of the menu
New icon guide — tap it to see what every dashboard icon means, with a Back to dashboard button
Tidier single-column dashboard — printer tiles are now square
Translations are a best-effort starting point; corrections are welcome

New "How pairing works" guide on launch (and in the menu) — explains that you pair once, that app updates keep your printers, and how to bring printers back after a reinstall (Restore from a backup, or MOONGATE_RESET_OWNER then re-pair). Tick "Don't show this again" to hide it
Fixed remote access over the internet — printers connect through the secure tunnel again (a v0.6.3 bug returned a server error when you were away from home). Update your Pi to get the fix
Restore is now honest about results — it tells you when printers actually reconnected vs when they still need a re-pair
Bug reports now include the remote (tunnel) connection result and your Pi's plugin version, so problems are quicker to diagnose
Restore now brings your printers back ONLINE after a reinstall or on a new phone — no re-pairing. Your backup carries a one-time code that re-links them to the freshly-installed app
Re-run the Pi installer so the printer recognises the restored app (needed for restore to reconnect)
Import config from the Add Printer screen — restore before you pair, handy when reinstalling
New "Report a problem" in the menu — sends a bug report with diagnostics (app, device, network, printer status) attached so issues are easier to fix
Plus a "Trouble pairing? Send a report" link on the Add Printer screen

Set a printer's address by hand — a new "Advanced" option when adding a printer, and an address field in each printer's edit dialog. Handy when your printer is behind a reverse proxy (Traefik, Caddy, NPM) or in Docker and the app can't find it automatically — enter the address you'd use to open its web page in a browser
Clearer pairing — scanning the QR is marked as the instant method; typing the GATE code is flagged as the slower alternative
Steadier first connection — a freshly-paired printer is less likely to show a premature "Offline", and recovers on its own when you return to the app

Fixed a printer staying on "Starting up…" forever when it was paired while powered off (or went offline right after pairing) — it now settles to "Offline" once unreachable, with a short grace window for one that's genuinely still booting
New optional App lock — protect Moongate with a PIN, plus fingerprint or face if your phone supports it (Menu → App lock)
The lock appears when you open the app; you can optionally re-lock after time in the background
Your PIN is stored encrypted on the device and wrong guesses are rate-limited
Slimmer app — removed unused VPN code, so Moongate no longer asks for VPN or special background permissions
Back up & restore your printer list to a file — "Back up config" / "Restore config" now use a file you choose instead of the clipboard, so it survives reinstalling the app
Fixed missing print progress and chamber temperature when viewing a printer over local Wi-Fi
Pairing is instant — scan the QR and the printer shows as Local right away
Remote (tunnel) access now syncs in the background; both icons appear once it's ready
No more sitting on "Starting up… waiting for first heartbeat" after you pair
New "Dashboard camera feed" setting — Raw / 1s / 3s / 5s (default 1s) to cut data use
Dashboard webcams pause while the app is in the background
Reinstalling or on a new phone? Run MOONGATE_RESET_OWNER on the Pi first, then re-pair
Re-run the Pi installer to enable instant pairing
Dashboard tile stays "Local" when your Pi's IP changes — even if the internet is down
Finds your Pi on the local network via mDNS (pairs with the v0.4.4 Pi advertisement)
Print controls now read the live LAN URL too — no stale-IP gap after DHCP changes
Falls through to v0.4.x behaviour when discovery doesn't resolve (different WiFi, multicast blocked, etc.)
Re-run the Pi installer to enable the Pi-side advertisement
Pi-side groundwork for LAN discovery — your Pi now advertises itself on the local network
No visible change yet on its own; pairs with the upcoming v0.5.0 app update
Re-run the Pi installer to pick up the change
Webcam tile now works on slow camera servers (stock RatRig Micron+ / uv4l-mjpeg)
Self-throttles to whatever the camera can deliver instead of cancelling its own fetches
No Pi-side change needed — just update the app
Re-pair after app reinstall just works — no more "already paired"
Pairing goes live within seconds, not up to 5 minutes
Mainsail through tunnel stays connected on cellular
Type the GATE code if the camera can't scan the QR
Dashboard tile webcam preview works again on LAN and tunnel
No reinstall on the Pi needed — just this app update
Hardened remote access — the tunnel URL alone grants nothing
Mainsail reachable through the tunnel from anywhere
Added 'Buy me a coffee' and this 'What's new'
Cloud-mediated pairing — no more URL sharing required
LAN-first routing for snappier home use
Cleaner remove / re-pair flow
Initial public release
Pi-issued JWT auth, dual-path local + tunnel
Mainsail / Fluidd WebView per printer
